name: Xero API
what_it_does: Cloud accounting API popular in Australia, New Zealand, and UK. Provides full double-entry accounting, invoicing, payroll, bank reconciliation, and financial reporting via REST API.
use_cases: Automating invoice creation, approval, and payment workflows
Syncing financial data between Xero and other business systems
Building accounting automation for businesses in AU/NZ/UK markets
Pulling financial reports for AI-driven financial analysis
Payroll automation and employee expense management
not_for: Inventory-heavy businesses needing deep warehouse management
best_when: An agent needs to automate accounting for AU/NZ/UK small to medium businesses, or any business already on Xero globally.
avoid_when: Your customers are primarily US small businesses (QuickBooks has higher adoption) or require enterprise-scale ERP.

auth.notes: OAuth2 with PKCE required. Access tokens expire in 30 minutes (shorter than most!), refresh tokens valid 60 days. Supports app store (single tenant) and partner (multi-tenant) apps. Scopes per API (accounting, payroll, assets, etc.).

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free developer demo company for testing
pricing.notes: API access included with any Xero subscription. Developer demo company is free. No per-API-call billing. App partner program free to join.

performance.rate_limits: 60 calls/minute per app per Xero org, 5000 calls/day per org

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Xero errors include ErrorNumber, Type, Message, and ValidationErrors array. Validation errors are field-level. Messages are reasonably clear but sometimes reference Xero-internal concepts.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency keys. Updates require current object for optimistic concurrency. Agents should fetch before updating to get current state.
known_agent_gotchas: 30-minute access token expiry is very short — agents need frequent token refresh
60 calls/minute limit is tight for agents processing batch operations
OAuth2 PKCE flow requires interactive user authorization — hard for fully autonomous agents
Tenant ID required in all requests — multi-tenant agents must track tenant context
Financial year varies by country — date ranges in queries must account for this
Archived records still count toward API responses unless explicitly filtered
OpenAPI spec available but generated SDKs have known edge cases
